name: Cairo
description: A sprawling, ancient megacity where the pyramids meet a chaotic, deeply alive urban culture. Young travelers come for history, dirt-cheap living, and a buzzing creative and cafe scene along the Nile.
vibe_summary: Relentless, dusty, and electric — millennia of history layered under honking traffic and late-night shisha lounges.
tax_notes: Moderate progressive income tax with relatively low capital gains, and foreign-sourced income is generally not taxed for residents.

name: Dahab
description: A laid-back Red Sea diving town that became a budget nomad haven with world-class reefs and the famous Blue Hole. Days are slow, cheap, and barefoot, drawing remote workers who want sun and sea without the resort crowds.
vibe_summary: Bohemian, barefoot, and salty — Bedouin cafes, dive shops, and desert mountains tumbling into turquoise water.
tax_notes: Egyptian residents pay moderate income tax, but foreign-sourced nomad income is typically untaxed in practice.

name: Addis Ababa
description: A high-altitude African capital with a temperate climate, booming cafe culture, and the continent's diplomatic hub energy. Young professionals find a fast-growing scene, incredible coffee, and a gateway to Ethiopia's wild landscapes.
vibe_summary: Cool mountain air, the birthplace of coffee, and a capital buzzing with pan-African ambition.
tax_notes: Relatively high progressive income tax rates with limited infrastructure for digital nomads.

name: Accra
description: Ghana's coastal capital is West Africa's most welcoming entry point, with English everywhere, Atlantic beaches, and a thriving Afrobeats and diaspora culture. The 'Year of Return' made it a magnet for young Black professionals and creatives.
vibe_summary: Warm, English-speaking, and full of Afrobeats energy — beach bars, art galleries, and diaspora reunions.
tax_notes: Progressive income tax up to 30% with moderate corporate rates and a growing but bureaucratic system.

name: Nairobi
description: East Africa's tech and startup capital, nicknamed 'Silicon Savannah', with a temperate highland climate and safari at the doorstep. Young professionals and nomads find a vibrant scene of co-working spaces, nightlife, and adventure.
vibe_summary: Silicon Savannah energy — startup hustle by day, rooftop bars and game parks within reach.
tax_notes: Income taxed up to 30% but Kenya offers a digital nomad visa, and foreign income rules can be favorable for short-stay remote workers.

name: Mauritius
description: A tropical Indian Ocean island with world-class beaches, low taxes, and a multicultural, stable society. Its Premium Travel Visa and business-friendly regime make it a favorite for nomads and entrepreneurs seeking sun and tax efficiency.
vibe_summary: Turquoise lagoons, lush mountains, and a tax-friendly island lifestyle blending African, Indian, and French cultures.
tax_notes: Flat 15% income and corporate tax with no capital gains tax, plus a free Premium Travel Visa make it highly attractive to nomads.

name: Casablanca
description: Morocco's economic powerhouse and largest city, a modern Atlantic port with Art Deco architecture and a fast business pulse. Young professionals find the country's best startup infrastructure alongside cafe culture and coastal escapes.
vibe_summary: Morocco's commercial engine — Art Deco facades, ocean-front corniche, and a buttoned-up business energy.
tax_notes: High top marginal income tax rates, though Morocco's easy 90-day entry and low cost of living draw many nomads.

name: Chefchaouen
description: The famous 'Blue City' tucked into the Rif Mountains, all cobalt-washed alleys and mountain serenity. A small, photogenic, slow-paced retreat popular with creatives and hikers seeking calm over hustle.
vibe_summary: A dreamy blue-painted medina in the Rif Mountains — slow, photogenic, and deeply tranquil.
tax_notes: Morocco's high marginal income tax applies, but the easy entry stamp and rock-bottom costs make it a budget-friendly stay.

name: Essaouira
description: A breezy Atlantic port town beloved for its windsurfing, walled medina, and laid-back arts scene. Cooler and calmer than Marrakech, it draws surfers, creatives, and nomads chasing wind and bohemian charm.
vibe_summary: Windswept ramparts, gnaoua music, and surf — a bohemian Atlantic counterpoint to inland Morocco.
tax_notes: Morocco's progressive tax tops out high, but most nomads stay visa-free on tourist entries and enjoy low living costs.

name: Marrakech
description: The Red City dazzles with its labyrinthine souks, riads, and Jemaa el-Fnaa's nightly spectacle, with the Atlas Mountains as a backdrop. A magnet for nomads, creatives, and party crowds drawn to its exotic energy and cheap luxury.
vibe_summary: Snake charmers, rooftop riads, and desert glamour — sensory overload framed by snow-capped Atlas peaks.
tax_notes: High marginal income tax for residents, but a popular nomad base thanks to easy tourist entry and very low costs.

name: Taghazout
description: A tiny Atlantic fishing village turned global surf and yoga mecca, with consistent point breaks and year-round sun. It's become one of Africa's premier surf-nomad spots, all surf camps, smoothie bowls, and co-working by the sea.
vibe_summary: Endless point breaks, yoga decks, and sunset surf sessions — Morocco's barefoot surf capital.
tax_notes: Morocco's top tax rates are high, but surf nomads typically stay on tourist entries and benefit from very cheap living.

name: Tangier
description: A storied port where the Mediterranean meets the Atlantic, just a ferry from Spain, long a haunt of writers and artists. Its mix of European and Moroccan culture, sea views, and improving infrastructure draws a creative, cosmopolitan crowd.
vibe_summary: Where Africa eyes Europe across the strait — a literary, sea-sprayed mix of medina chaos and Spanish flair.
tax_notes: Standard high Moroccan income tax applies, but proximity to Europe and cheap living make it a convenient nomad base.

name: Lagos
description: Africa's largest and most frenetic megacity, the beating heart of Afrobeats, Nollywood, and a booming fintech scene. Intense and unfiltered, it rewards the bold with unmatched cultural energy and entrepreneurial opportunity.
vibe_summary: Pure adrenaline — gridlock, Afrobeats, fintech hustle, and a creative scene exporting culture worldwide.
tax_notes: Moderate personal income tax with low capital gains, but heavy bureaucracy and security concerns offset the savings.

name: Seychelles
description: An archipelago of granite-fringed beaches and turquoise water, one of the planet's most pristine tropical destinations. Its Workcation Visa and tax-light regime appeal to nomads who can afford its premium island lifestyle.
vibe_summary: Postcard granite boulders, powder-white sand, and crystalline water — barefoot luxury in the Indian Ocean.
tax_notes: Flat 15% income tax and no capital gains tax, with an accessible Workcation visa, though the cost of living is high.

name: Cape Town
description: Wedged between Table Mountain and two oceans, Cape Town is Africa's most stunning city and a top global nomad hub. World-class wine, beaches, hiking, and a thriving creative scene combine with a strong dollar advantage for Westerners.
vibe_summary: Table Mountain looming over surf beaches and vineyards — jaw-dropping nature with cosmopolitan creative energy.
tax_notes: High personal income tax rates, but South Africa launched a remote-work visa and the weak rand stretches foreign income far.

name: Johannesburg
description: South Africa's economic engine and largest city, a high-altitude hub of business, art, and a fast-evolving urban culture. Edgier than Cape Town, 'Joburg' offers the continent's deepest job market and a raw, reinventing creative scene.
vibe_summary: Gold-rush grit turned creative capital — corporate towers, township energy, and a relentless reinvention.
tax_notes: High income tax rates apply, though South Africa's remote-work visa and favorable exchange rate appeal to nomads.

name: Zanzibar
description: A spice-trading island of white-sand beaches, Swahili culture, and the historic Stone Town. It has exploded as a nomad and beach hotspot, with growing co-working scenes in Paje and Jambiani alongside kitesurfing and turquoise lagoons.
vibe_summary: Stone Town's spice-scented alleys giving way to kitesurf beaches and palm-fringed Indian Ocean lagoons.
tax_notes: Tanzanian income tax runs up to 30%, but most beach nomads stay on tourist visas and enjoy low daily costs.

name: Kigali
description: Africa's cleanest and safest capital, a hilly, orderly city positioning itself as an East African tech and conference hub. Its temperate climate, low crime, and pro-business reforms attract a new wave of remote workers and entrepreneurs.
vibe_summary: Spotless, safe, and rising — green hills, smooth roads, and an unexpectedly calm African capital.
tax_notes: Income tax tops at 30% but capital gains are low, and Rwanda's streamlined visa-on-arrival and clean governance ease the admin burden.

name: Dakar
description: Senegal's vibrant Atlantic capital on the westernmost tip of Africa, known for its music, surf, and Franco-Wolof culture. A growing creative and tech scene, plus consistent surf and a coastal setting, draw the adventurous nomad.
vibe_summary: Mbalax rhythms, Atlantic surf breaks, and the bustling crossroads of French and West African culture.
tax_notes: High marginal income tax rates, though visa-free entry for many Westerners and low costs offset the burden for short stays.

name: Diani Beach
description: A long stretch of white sand on Kenya's south coast, repeatedly voted Africa's leading beach destination. Combined with Kenya's digital nomad visa, it's become a laid-back coastal base of co-working spots, kitesurfing, and warm Indian Ocean water.
vibe_summary: Powder-soft sand, palm-lined Indian Ocean, and a slow-paced coastal nomad scene south of Mombasa.
tax_notes: Kenyan income tax tops at 30%, but a new digital nomad visa and cheap coastal living make Diani attractive for remote workers.

name: Windhoek
description: Namibia's tidy, high-altitude capital is a calm, safe gateway to the country's surreal deserts and wildlife. Small but well-organized, it suits travelers craving wide-open space, dark skies, and easy English over big-city buzz.
vibe_summary: A quiet, orderly highland capital — German colonial echoes and a launchpad into Namibia's vast desert wilderness.
tax_notes: High top income tax but no general capital gains tax, and Namibia introduced a nomad visa with English widely spoken.

name: Hurghada
description: A Red Sea resort city built for sun, diving, and watersports, with year-round warmth and rock-bottom prices. Its expanding nomad and kitesurf scene offers cheap beachfront living and easy access to vibrant coral reefs.
vibe_summary: Sun-soaked Red Sea resort strip — coral reefs, kitesurf bays, and cheap beachfront living all year.
tax_notes: Moderate Egyptian income tax with foreign income generally untaxed, making cheap Red Sea living appealing to nomads.

name: Stellenbosch
description: A leafy university and wine town in the Cape Winelands, blending Cape Dutch heritage with a youthful, intellectual buzz. Surrounded by vineyards and mountains, it offers a safer, slower, scenic alternative to nearby Cape Town.
vibe_summary: Oak-lined streets, Cape Dutch gables, and vineyards against mountains — a youthful, wine-soaked university town.
tax_notes: South Africa's high income tax applies, but the remote-work visa and favorable rand make the winelands affordable for foreigners.

name: Bahrain
description: A liberal Gulf island state with a relaxed social scene, no income tax, and easy access to Saudi Arabia via the causeway. Young professionals find a more laid-back, English-friendly Gulf alternative to Dubai with a lively brunch-and-bar culture.
vibe_summary: Easygoing Gulf island where desert heat meets a surprisingly liberal nightlife and a tight expat circuit.
tax_notes: No personal income tax and no capital gains tax, making it highly attractive for expats and remote earners.

name: Phnom Penh
description: Cambodia's chaotic, fast-developing capital is cheap, easy on visas, and full of riverside bars and a growing remote-work crowd. Young expats are drawn by rock-bottom costs and an unpolished, anything-goes energy.
vibe_summary: Gritty riverside capital that's dirt cheap, frenetic, and wide open to anyone willing to roll with the chaos.
tax_notes: Easy long-stay business visas and lax enforcement on foreign income make it popular with nomads despite a 20% nominal rate.

name: Siem Reap
description: Gateway to Angkor Wat, this small town pairs world-class temples with a backpacker-turned-nomad scene of cheap cafes and Pub Street nightlife. It's affordable, easygoing, and increasingly a quiet remote-work base.
vibe_summary: Temple-town charm with cheap cafes by day and a buzzing Pub Street by night.
tax_notes: Cheap renewable visas and minimal scrutiny of foreign-earned income suit long-stay nomads.

name: Hong Kong
description: A hyper-dense global finance hub where skyscrapers meet hiking trails and island beaches. Young professionals get world-class transit, dizzying nightlife, and low taxes, offset by some of the priciest rent on earth.
vibe_summary: Vertical, electric financial megacity where neon harbors sit minutes from green mountain trails.
tax_notes: Flat low salaries tax capped near 15% and zero capital gains tax make it a tax haven, but residency visas are hard to get.

name: Tbilisi
description: Georgia's atmospheric capital offers a one-year visa-free stay, dirt-cheap living, and a booming nomad scene amid old-town balconies and wine bars. Mountains, sulfur baths, and a thriving cafe culture make it a budget remote-work darling.
vibe_summary: Bohemian Caucasus capital of crumbling balconies, natural wine, and a fast-growing nomad crowd.
tax_notes: One-year visa-free stays plus a 1% small-business tax regime and no tax on foreign-source income make it a nomad tax favorite.

name: Goa
description: India's beach state blends palm-fringed coastline with a legendary trance-party legacy and a now-thriving nomad and yoga scene. Cheap, sunny, and laid-back, it draws remote workers to its north-coast cafes and full-moon parties.
vibe_summary: Palm-lined Indian coast where rave history, beach shacks, and yoga retreats all collide.
tax_notes: India's high personal rates apply to residents, but most nomads stay on tourist visas and pay tax at home.

name: Pondicherry
description: A former French colony on India's southeast coast, with pastel colonial streets, the Auroville spiritual community, and a calm seaside promenade. It draws a wellness-minded, slower-paced crowd seeking culture and cheap coastal living.
vibe_summary: Pastel French-colonial seaside town with an Auroville-flavored spiritual, slow-living calm.
tax_notes: India's high marginal rates apply to residents, though most foreign visitors remain on tourist visas and pay tax abroad.

name: Udaipur
description: The romantic 'City of Lakes' in Rajasthan, ringed by palaces, hilltop forts, and shimmering waters. It's a culturally rich, affordable base for travelers drawn to heritage, photography, and a slower royal-era ambiance.
vibe_summary: Whitewashed lake-palace city steeped in Rajasthani royalty, sunsets, and rooftop chai.
tax_notes: India's high resident tax rates apply, but most visitors stay on tourist visas and are taxed in their home country.

name: Bali
description: The archetypal digital-nomad island, blending rice-terrace serenity, surf, and a massive remote-work community across Canggu, Ubud, and the south. Affordable, wellness-obsessed, and endlessly social, it's the global poster child for the laptop-and-beach life.
vibe_summary: Tropical nomad paradise of rice paddies, surf breaks, and a sprawling wellness-and-coworking scene.
tax_notes: Indonesia's new nomad-friendly visas let many stay tax-free on foreign income, though formal residents face rates up to 35%.

name: Canggu
description: Bali's beachside nomad capital, packed with coworking spaces, smoothie bowls, surf breaks, and beach clubs. It's the epicenter of the remote-work-meets-party lifestyle, drawing a dense young international crowd.
vibe_summary: Surf-and-laptop village where beach clubs, coworking lofts, and scooter traffic blur into one nomad hub.
tax_notes: Most nomads remain on tourist or remote-work visas and pay no local tax on foreign income; residents face rates up to 35%.

name: Lombok
description: Bali's quieter neighbor, with empty beaches, the towering Mount Rinjani volcano, and the surf-and-party Gili Islands offshore. It draws those seeking a cheaper, less-developed tropical base with serious outdoor adventure.
vibe_summary: Wilder, mellower island of empty surf beaches and a giant volcano looming over the rice fields.
tax_notes: Nomads typically stay visa-based and untaxed locally on foreign income; Indonesian residents face up to 35%.

name: Ubud
description: Bali's inland cultural and wellness heart, surrounded by rice terraces, yoga shalas, and vegan cafes. It draws a spiritually-minded, slower nomad crowd seeking retreats, healing arts, and lush jungle calm.
vibe_summary: Jungle-and-rice-terrace wellness sanctuary of yoga shalas, healers, and vegan cafes.
tax_notes: Foreign-income nomads on tourist or remote visas pay no local tax; Indonesian residents face rates up to 35%.

name: Yogyakarta
description: Java's cultural and student capital, home to the Borobudur and Prambanan temples, a vibrant arts scene, and very low costs. Its youthful, creative energy and affordability make it an under-the-radar base for culture-seekers.
vibe_summary: Java's artsy student city of ancient temples, batik workshops, and cheap creative energy.
tax_notes: Nomads on tourist visas pay no local tax on foreign income; formal Indonesian residents face up to 35%.

name: Fukuoka
description: Japan's most startup-friendly city, with a compact, walkable layout, beaches, ramen culture, and a relaxed pace versus Tokyo. A startup-visa program and lower costs make it a rising base for entrepreneurial young expats.
vibe_summary: Laid-back, walkable Japanese port city famed for tonkotsu ramen and a budding startup streak.
tax_notes: Japan's progressive rates are moderately high, though Fukuoka's startup visa eases entry for founders.

name: Kyoto
description: Japan's ancient capital of temples, geisha districts, and zen gardens, balancing deep tradition with a refined modern lifestyle. It draws those seeking culture, walkability, and seasonal beauty over big-city pace.
vibe_summary: Temple-laden former imperial capital where moss gardens and machiya townhouses meet seasonal ritual.
tax_notes: Japan's progressive income tax is moderately high, with limited long-stay visa options for remote workers.

name: Okinawa
description: Japan's subtropical island prefecture, with white-sand beaches, coral reefs, and one of the world's famous Blue Zone longevity cultures. It offers a slower, beachy Japanese lifestyle far from the mainland's intensity.
vibe_summary: Subtropical Blue Zone islands of coral reefs, longevity living, and a distinct laid-back island culture.
tax_notes: Japan's standard progressive rates apply, with few dedicated long-stay options for remote workers.

name: Osaka
description: Japan's brash, food-obsessed second city, famed for street eats, neon nightlife, and a friendlier, more outgoing vibe than Tokyo. It pairs big-city energy with lower costs and superb transit.
vibe_summary: Neon-soaked street-food capital with a loud, friendly, eat-till-you-drop personality.
tax_notes: Japan's progressive income tax is moderately high, with limited long-stay visa routes for nomads.

name: Tokyo
description: The world's largest metro, a hyper-efficient sprawl of neon, Michelin-starred food, and endless subcultures. It offers unmatched safety, transit, and energy for young people who thrive on big-city intensity.
vibe_summary: Endless neon megacity of immaculate trains, vending-machine convenience, and infinite micro-scenes.
tax_notes: Japan's progressive rates climb to 45% at the top, though a digital-nomad visa now allows six-month stays.

name: Aqaba
description: Jordan's Red Sea port and special economic zone, with world-class diving, desert-meets-sea scenery, and low taxes. It's a sunny coastal base near Wadi Rum and Petra for adventure-minded travelers.
vibe_summary: Sun-baked Red Sea diving town where desert dunes meet coral reefs and tax-free shopping.
tax_notes: Aqaba's special economic zone offers reduced rates and no capital gains tax, easing the burden for businesses and expats.

name: Kuala Lumpur
description: Malaysia's skyscraper-studded, multicultural capital offers world-class street food, modern infrastructure, and an affordable, English-friendly base. Its DE Rantau nomad visa and low costs make it a rising remote-work hub.
vibe_summary: Affordable, multicultural skyline city where Malay, Chinese, and Indian street-food worlds collide.
tax_notes: No capital gains tax and a territorial system that exempts most foreign income, plus the DE Rantau nomad visa.

name: Langkawi
description: A duty-free tropical archipelago off Malaysia's northwest coast, with rainforest, cable-car peaks, and quiet beaches. It's a low-key, affordable island escape with tax-free shopping and a slower rhythm.
vibe_summary: Duty-free jungle-and-beach island of mangrove tours, cable cars, and unhurried tropical calm.
tax_notes: Duty-free status plus Malaysia's territorial tax and no capital gains tax keep costs and taxes low.

name: Penang
description: A UNESCO-listed heritage island famed as Malaysia's street-food capital, blending colonial George Town with beaches and a strong expat-retiree-and-nomad community. Affordable, English-friendly, and culturally rich.
vibe_summary: Heritage island of George Town shophouses, legendary hawker food, and a settled expat scene.
tax_notes: Malaysia's territorial tax exempts most foreign income and there's no capital gains tax, aiding expats and the MM2H visa crowd.

name: Muscat
description: Oman's low-rise, mountain-backed capital offers dramatic desert-and-sea scenery, safety, and tax-free living without the glitz of its Gulf neighbors. It draws those wanting authentic Arabian culture and outdoor adventure.
vibe_summary: Whitewashed, mountain-ringed Gulf capital of forts, wadis, and a calm, traditional pace.
tax_notes: No personal income tax and no capital gains tax make Oman highly favorable for expats and remote earners.

name: Cebu
description: The Philippines' second city, a fast-growing urban hub with nearby diving, island hopping, and whale sharks. English-fluent and affordable, it's a practical base for nomads who want city amenities plus quick beach access.
vibe_summary: Buzzing port-city base for island hopping, diving, and dirt-cheap, English-easy living.
tax_notes: Foreign income generally isn't taxed for non-resident expats, and easy visa extensions suit long-stay nomads.

name: Dumaguete
description: A small, walkable university town on Negros island known as the 'City of Gentle People,' with cheap living, diving, and a friendly expat scene. It draws those wanting a calm, affordable, English-speaking coastal base.
vibe_summary: Mellow seaside university town of diving, cheap living, and famously gentle locals.
tax_notes: Non-resident expats are generally untaxed on foreign income, with easy renewable tourist visas.

name: Palawan
description: Routinely voted among the world's most beautiful islands, with the lagoons of El Nido, Coron's wrecks, and untouched jungle coastline. It's a bucket-list tropical paradise for divers and beach-lovers seeking raw nature.
vibe_summary: Postcard archipelago of hidden lagoons, limestone cliffs, and some of the planet's clearest water.
tax_notes: Non-resident expats generally pay no local tax on foreign income; visas are easily extended.

name: Siargao
description: The Philippines' surf capital, a teardrop island of palm forests, Cloud 9 waves, and a fast-growing nomad-and-surfer scene. Laid-back and increasingly social, it's become a magnet for remote workers chasing waves.
vibe_summary: Palm-forested surf island where Cloud 9 barrels, motorbike days, and a growing nomad scene set the rhythm.
tax_notes: Non-resident nomads generally owe no local tax on foreign income, with easily renewable tourist visas.

name: Doha
description: Qatar's ultramodern, ultra-safe capital, a tax-free Gulf hub of futuristic skylines, museums, and a major airline crossroads. It draws young professionals chasing high salaries and a polished, safe expat lifestyle.
vibe_summary: Gleaming, tax-free desert metropolis of futuristic towers, world-class museums, and immaculate safety.
tax_notes: No personal income tax and no capital gains tax make Doha extremely attractive for high earners.

name: Jeddah
description: Saudi Arabia's Red Sea gateway and most relaxed major city, with a historic old town, a long corniche, and growing diving and arts scenes. Rapid liberalization and tax-free pay draw young professionals to its coastal energy.
vibe_summary: Red Sea port city of coral-stone old quarters, a sprawling corniche, and a fast-loosening social scene.
tax_notes: No personal income tax and no capital gains tax for individuals make Saudi Arabia financially appealing for expats.

name: Riyadh
description: Saudi Arabia's fast-modernizing desert capital, the heart of Vision 2030 with booming construction, a rising startup scene, and tax-free salaries. It draws ambitious young professionals to a rapidly opening, high-paying market.
vibe_summary: Sprawling desert capital remaking itself at breakneck speed around towers, megaprojects, and tax-free ambition.
tax_notes: No personal income tax and no capital gains tax for individuals make Riyadh highly attractive for high earners.

name: Singapore
description: A hyper-efficient global city-state where remote workers enjoy world-class infrastructure, safety, and food. It is the cleanest, most connected base in Asia for ambitious young professionals, though it comes at a premium price.
vibe_summary: Futuristic, spotless, and relentlessly convenient — hawker stalls under glittering skyscrapers.
tax_notes: Low progressive income tax, no capital gains tax, and a business-friendly regime make it attractive for high earners.

name: Seoul
description: A dense, electric megacity with the fastest internet on earth, 24-hour cafes, and an obsessive food and beauty culture. Young professionals come for the energy, nightlife, and a startup scene backed by serious tech infrastructure.
vibe_summary: Neon-lit, sleepless, and trend-obsessed — a city that reinvents itself every season.
tax_notes: Moderate-to-high progressive income tax, though a new digital nomad visa eases longer stays for remote workers.

name: Taipei
description: A friendly, affordable East Asian capital famous for night markets, mountains within reach, and excellent healthcare. It blends big-city convenience with genuine warmth, making it a quietly beloved nomad base.
vibe_summary: Easygoing and food-obsessed — steamy night markets ringed by green peaks.
tax_notes: Reasonable income tax with no tax on most local securities gains and a gold card visa for skilled professionals.

name: Bangkok
description: Asia's nomad capital — cheap, chaotic, and endlessly stimulating, with luxury condos, street food, and rooftop bars on every block. The remote-work infrastructure and flight connectivity make it a default Southeast Asian hub.
vibe_summary: Sensory overload in the best way — street woks, skytrains, and rooftop neon.
tax_notes: Foreign income remitted in a later year is often untaxed, and a long-term resident visa offers tax perks for remote workers.

name: Chiang Mai
description: The original digital-nomad mecca — ultra-cheap, laid-back, and surrounded by mountains and temples. Co-working spaces, cafes, and a massive remote-work community make it the easiest soft landing in Asia.
vibe_summary: Mellow, cheap, and leafy — temple bells, latte art, and misty hills.
tax_notes: Same favorable Thai remittance rules and nomad-friendly visas, with living costs low enough that tax matters little.

name: Koh Phangan
description: A tropical island split between the wild Full Moon Party scene and a booming wellness-and-yoga community. It draws a young, spiritual nomad crowd chasing beaches, breathwork, and barefoot freedom.
vibe_summary: Jungle, full moons, and ecstatic dance — equal parts party and ashram.
tax_notes: Thai remittance-based taxation is lenient for nomads earning foreign income while island-based.

name: Koh Samui
description: A more polished resort island with palm-lined beaches, beach clubs, and a growing remote-work scene. It offers tropical comfort with its own airport and decent infrastructure for longer stays.
vibe_summary: Glossy resort island — coconut groves, beach clubs, and easy sunsets.
tax_notes: Standard favorable Thai rules apply, with foreign-earned income often outside the local tax net.

name: Krabi
description: A dramatic coastal province of limestone karsts, hidden beaches, and world-class rock climbing. It suits adventurous young travelers who want nature and tropical beauty over nightlife.
vibe_summary: Towering karsts over turquoise water — climber's paradise and beach-hopper's dream.
tax_notes: Thailand's remittance-based system keeps foreign income largely untaxed for short-to-medium stays.

name: Pai
description: A tiny mountain town beloved by backpackers and slow-travel nomads for its hippie cafes, hot springs, and lazy river valley. It is cheap, scenic, and intentionally off the fast lane.
vibe_summary: Bohemian mountain hideaway — hammocks, hot springs, and reggae on the river.
tax_notes: Favorable Thai remittance rules make it a low-cost, low-tax base for foreign-income earners.

name: Phuket
description: Thailand's largest island combines beach resorts, a buzzing nightlife strip, and a maturing nomad and wellness scene. With its own international airport, it balances party energy and longer-stay comfort.
vibe_summary: Big-island energy — beach clubs by day, neon Bangla Road by night.
tax_notes: Thai remittance-based taxation and nomad visas make it tax-light for foreign earners.

name: Da Nang
description: A clean, beachfront city that has become Vietnam's premier nomad hub, with cheap living, a long sandy coast, and mountains nearby. It offers modern comfort and a strong remote-work community at rock-bottom prices.
vibe_summary: Beach-city ease — surf at dawn, banh mi for lunch, riverside neon at night.
tax_notes: Most nomads stay on tourist or business e-visas and pay no local tax on foreign-sourced income.

name: Hanoi
description: Vietnam's atmospheric capital, where motorbike chaos, lakeside cafes, and an incredible street-food culture draw curious young travelers. It is gritty, deeply historic, and extraordinarily affordable.
vibe_summary: Old-quarter chaos and lakeside calm — egg coffee, scooters, and shophouse charm.
tax_notes: Foreign income generally goes untaxed for nomads on tourist or e-visas not registered as tax residents.

name: Ho Chi Minh City
description: Vietnam's frenetic commercial heart, packed with rooftop bars, startups, and a fast-growing professional scene. The energy, food, and low cost make it a magnet for ambitious young expats.
vibe_summary: Round-the-clock hustle — motorbike rivers, rooftop cocktails, and street-food empires.
tax_notes: Nomads on tourist or business visas typically pay no Vietnamese tax on foreign-sourced earnings.

name: Hoi An
description: A lantern-lit UNESCO town of tailor shops, riverside cafes, and nearby beaches that charms slow-travel nomads. It is calm, photogenic, and walkable in a way rare for Vietnam.
vibe_summary: Lantern glow on an old river town — bicycles, silk tailors, and rice-paddy sunsets.
tax_notes: Foreign income remains untaxed locally for nomads who are not Vietnamese tax residents.

name: Nha Trang
description: A beach resort city with a long palm-lined bay, diving, and a relaxed coastal pace. It offers cheap seaside living with enough infrastructure for medium-term stays.
vibe_summary: Easy beach-resort rhythm — long bay, fresh seafood, and warm sea breezes.
tax_notes: Standard Vietnamese rules leave foreign-earned income untaxed for non-resident nomads.

name: Almaty
description: Kazakhstan's leafy former capital sits at the foot of soaring mountains, offering skiing, hiking, and a surprisingly hip cafe and tech scene. It is an affordable, under-the-radar base for adventurous remote workers.
vibe_summary: Tree-lined boulevards beneath snow-capped peaks — Soviet bones, modern cafes.
tax_notes: A flat 10% personal income tax and visa-free entry for many nationalities make it quietly tax-friendly.

name: Yerevan
description: An ancient, walkable capital with a buzzing cafe culture, cheap living, and a big influx of remote tech workers. Easy banking and long visa-free stays have made it a Caucasus nomad favorite.
vibe_summary: Pink-stone city of cafes and cognac — ancient, warm, and unexpectedly cosmopolitan.
tax_notes: 180-day visa-free stays, easy banking, and a low micro-business tax regime attract remote founders.

name: Baku
description: A windswept Caspian capital where flame-shaped towers rise over a medieval old town. Oil wealth has built a sleek, modern city that remains affordable and intriguing for adventurous travelers.
vibe_summary: Caspian glamour meets old-town stone — futuristic towers over ancient walls.
tax_notes: A modest 14% income tax applies, though long-stay options for Westerners are more limited.

name: Colombo
description: Sri Lanka's coastal commercial capital blends colonial architecture, a growing cafe scene, and easy access to the island's surf coast. It is cheap, tropical, and increasingly on the nomad radar.
vibe_summary: Tropical port city — colonial verandas, sea breeze, and spicy seafood curries.
tax_notes: Nomads on tourist visas owe no local tax on foreign income, though local rates have risen recently.

name: Kathmandu
description: A chaotic, deeply spiritual Himalayan capital of temples, prayer flags, and trekking outfitters. It is the gateway to the world's greatest mountains and a magnet for adventurers on a budget.
vibe_summary: Incense, prayer wheels, and dust — a sacred basecamp beneath the Himalayas.
tax_notes: Tourist-visa nomads pay no Nepali tax on foreign income, and living costs are among the world's lowest.

name: Pokhara
description: A serene lakeside town beneath the Annapurna range, famed for paragliding, trekking, and slow recovery days. It is calmer and cleaner than Kathmandu, ideal for outdoorsy budget travelers.
vibe_summary: Mirror-still lake under snow giants — paragliders drift over a mellow trekker town.
tax_notes: Foreign income is untaxed for tourist-visa nomads, with extremely low living costs throughout.

name: Luang Prabang
description: A tranquil UNESCO town of gilded temples, riverside cafes, and morning alms processions at the meeting of two rivers. It rewards slow travelers seeking calm, culture, and waterfalls over nightlife.
vibe_summary: Saffron robes at dawn by the Mekong — serene, golden, and gently timeless.
tax_notes: Tourist-visa nomads pay no local tax on foreign income in this very low-cost town.

name: Vientiane
description: Laos's sleepy riverside capital mixes French colonial architecture, Buddhist temples, and a relaxed Mekong-side cafe culture. It is quiet and cheap, suiting nomads who want calm over buzz.
vibe_summary: Languid Mekong capital — baguettes, temples, and unhurried riverside evenings.
tax_notes: Nomads on tourist visas owe no Lao tax on foreign-sourced income.

name: Bengaluru
description: India's Silicon Valley, a green and temperate tech metropolis bursting with startups, craft breweries, and young professionals. It pairs a world-class engineering scene with a famously pleasant climate.
vibe_summary: India's startup engine — pub culture, IT campuses, and surprisingly cool evenings.
tax_notes: India taxes residents heavily, but nomads on e-visas pay no local tax on foreign income.

name: Mumbai
description: India's financial and entertainment capital, a coastal megacity of relentless ambition, Bollywood glamour, and extreme contrasts. It is intense and overwhelming, but unmatched for energy and opportunity.
vibe_summary: The city that never sleeps — Arabian Sea promenades, Bollywood dreams, and chaos.
tax_notes: High resident tax rates apply, but foreign income is untaxed for nomads on tourist or e-visas.

name: Busan
description: South Korea's laid-back coastal second city, where beaches, seafood markets, and mountains meet a modern skyline. It offers Korean infrastructure and culture at a slower, sunnier pace than Seoul.
vibe_summary: Beach-and-mountain port city — fish markets, surf, and skyscrapers by the sea.
tax_notes: Same Korean tax regime as Seoul, with the digital nomad visa opening longer stays for remote workers.

name: Batumi
description: A quirky Black Sea resort city with casinos, palm-lined promenades, and a famously easy tax and visa setup. It draws nomads with year-round residency ease and cheap seaside living.
vibe_summary: Eccentric Black Sea boardwalk — palm trees, neon casinos, and pebble beaches.
tax_notes: Georgia's 1-year visa-free stays and a 1% small-business tax regime make it a tax haven for nomads.

name: Da Lat
description: A cool, pine-clad highland town nicknamed Vietnam's 'City of Eternal Spring,' famed for flowers, waterfalls, and coffee farms. Its mild climate and romantic scenery offer a refreshing escape from the lowland heat.
vibe_summary: Misty pine hills and flower farms — cool air, French villas, and endless coffee.
tax_notes: Foreign income stays untaxed locally for nomads who are not registered Vietnamese tax residents.

name: Tirana
description: Albania's colorful, fast-changing capital has become a budget-friendly nomad pick with a year-long visa-free stay for many Westerners. Cheap rents, a warming cafe scene, and proximity to the Adriatic make it an under-the-radar base.
vibe_summary: Gritty-meets-optimistic Balkan capital painted in bold colors, buzzing with cheap espresso and new energy.
tax_notes: Flat-ish low rates and a generous one-year visa-free stay make Albania attractive, though tax residency rules tighten after 183 days.

name: Vienna
description: Repeatedly ranked the world's most livable city, Vienna pairs imperial grandeur with flawless transit, green space, and a deep cultural calendar. Safe and elegant, it's a polished base for professionals who want quality of life over hustle.
vibe_summary: Stately coffee-house elegance and effortless livability wrapped in Habsburg architecture.
tax_notes: High personal taxes fund excellent public services, with a 27.5% flat rate on capital gains.

name: Brussels
description: The EU's de facto capital is a bilingual, international hub with a strong job market and surprisingly affordable rents for its tier. Beer culture, Art Nouveau, and easy rail links to Paris, Amsterdam, and London anchor it.
vibe_summary: Underrated, multilingual EU crossroads with great beer, comics, and connections everywhere.
tax_notes: Among Europe's highest income taxes, but private individuals pay no capital gains tax on normal investments.

name: Dubrovnik
description: The walled "Pearl of the Adriatic" pairs stunning medieval stonework with turquoise sea and Croatia's nomad visa. It's small and tourist-heavy in summer but breathtaking and walkable.
vibe_summary: A honey-stone medieval city wrapped in walls above a dazzling blue Adriatic.
tax_notes: Croatia's digital nomad visa exempts qualifying remote income from local tax, a major draw.

name: Split
description: Built around a living Roman palace, Split is a lively coastal city that doubles as a launchpad to the Dalmatian islands. Croatia's nomad visa and a growing remote crowd make it a Mediterranean favorite.
vibe_summary: Ancient Roman palace turned buzzing seaside old town with islands at its doorstep.
tax_notes: Croatia's digital nomad visa exempts qualifying remote income from local tax, a major draw.

name: Prague
description: Fairy-tale architecture, cheap world-class beer, and a deep expat infrastructure make Prague one of Europe's enduring nomad capitals. It's safe, central, and affordable relative to Western Europe.
vibe_summary: Gothic spires and riverside beer gardens with a buzzing, affordable expat undercurrent.
tax_notes: Relatively moderate flat-ish income tax and a popular Zivnostensky freelance license attract remote workers.

name: Copenhagen
description: A design-obsessed, bike-first capital that tops happiness and quality-of-life rankings. Expensive but impeccably run, with strong startups and a famous food scene.
vibe_summary: Hygge-perfect, bike-everywhere design capital where everything just works.
tax_notes: One of the world's highest tax burdens, funding extensive welfare and services.

name: Tallinn
description: A medieval old town fused with one of Europe's most advanced digital states, Tallinn pioneered the e-Residency and a dedicated nomad visa. Safe, online-friendly, and startup-rich for its size.
vibe_summary: Storybook Hanseatic old town running on cutting-edge e-government rails.
tax_notes: A flat 20% tax and famously frictionless e-Residency make company setup and admin trivial.

name: Helsinki
description: A clean, design-forward Nordic capital ringed by sea and forest, with saunas as a way of life. Safe and high-functioning, with a quietly strong tech scene.
vibe_summary: Crisp Baltic-sea design city where saunas, forests, and minimalism rule.
tax_notes: High progressive income tax typical of the Nordics, with strong public services in return.

name: Bordeaux
description: A graceful wine-country city of golden 18th-century facades, two hours from Paris by TGV and close to Atlantic surf beaches. It blends laid-back living with real culinary and cultural depth.
vibe_summary: Elegant stone-fronted wine capital with surf coast and slow, good living.
tax_notes: High French taxes and notoriously heavy paperwork, softened by excellent public services.

name: Lyon
description: France's gastronomic capital sits between two rivers with Alps and Provence within easy reach. It offers big-city amenities and a strong economy at a calmer pace than Paris.
vibe_summary: Riverside food capital with Renaissance lanes and the Alps an hour away.
tax_notes: High French taxes and heavy bureaucracy, offset by strong healthcare and infrastructure.

name: Marseille
description: France's gritty, sun-soaked Mediterranean port is its most diverse and unfiltered big city, fringed by the dramatic Calanques. Cheaper and edgier than the Riviera, with a strong creative streak.
vibe_summary: Sun-baked, multicultural port city with rugged sea cliffs and raw energy.
tax_notes: Standard high French tax regime with heavy administrative friction for newcomers.

name: Nice
description: The capital of the French Riviera offers palm-lined seafront promenades, near-Italian warmth, and quick access to Monaco and the Alps. Glamorous and well-connected, if pricey.
vibe_summary: Belle Epoque Riviera glamour on a turquoise bay between mountains and Monaco.
tax_notes: Standard high French taxation, with proximity to tax-haven Monaco a notable contrast.

name: Paris
description: An unrivaled global capital of art, food, fashion, and ideas, dense with culture and a serious startup scene. Expensive and bureaucratic, but endlessly stimulating and beautifully walkable.
vibe_summary: Iconic, intense city of light where every corner is cinema and culture.
tax_notes: High French income and social taxes paired with dense red tape, but world-class services.

name: Berlin
description: Europe's creative and club capital is a magnet for artists, founders, and nomads drawn by its openness and (still relatively) affordable edge. A huge international community and a leading startup scene make it endlessly absorbing.
vibe_summary: Raw, free-spirited creative hub where techno, art, and startups collide.
tax_notes: High German income tax and slow bureaucracy, but a freelancer (Freiberufler) visa pathway is popular.

name: Munich
description: Bavaria's prosperous capital pairs a powerhouse economy with beer gardens, the Alps on the doorstep, and exceptional safety and cleanliness. Pricey but polished, with strong jobs in tech and engineering.
vibe_summary: Affluent, orderly Alpine-edge city of beer gardens and big employers.
tax_notes: High German taxes, offset by strong wages and excellent infrastructure.

name: Athens
description: An ancient city in the middle of a gritty creative revival, Athens offers cheap rents, endless sun, and ferries to the islands. Its nomad visa and tax incentives have pulled in a fast-growing remote crowd.
vibe_summary: Sun-blasted ancient capital with a raw, rising street-art and rooftop-bar scene.
tax_notes: A 50% income-tax break for new tax residents makes Greece unusually attractive for relocating remote workers.

name: Crete
description: Greece's largest island blends dramatic coastlines, mountain villages, and a long warm season with a relaxed pace. The nomad visa and low costs make it a slow-living Mediterranean base.
vibe_summary: Wild, sun-drenched island of beaches, gorges, and unhurried village life.
tax_notes: Greece's 50% tax break for new residents and a nomad visa offset otherwise moderate rates.

name: Greek Islands
description: The Aegean and Ionian islands offer whitewashed villages, turquoise water, and a famously slow, sun-soaked rhythm. Best as a seasonal base, with the nomad visa enabling longer stays.
vibe_summary: Whitewashed cliffside villages above impossibly blue Aegean water.
tax_notes: Greece's new-resident tax incentives and nomad visa make seasonal or longer island stays viable.

name: Thessaloniki
description: Greece's second city is a youthful, food-loving seaside university town with a more local, affordable feel than Athens. Its waterfront, nightlife, and student energy make it a sleeper nomad pick.
vibe_summary: Easygoing seafront student city with great food and unpretentious nightlife.
tax_notes: Greece's new-resident 50% tax break and nomad visa apply, easing otherwise moderate rates.

name: Budapest
description: Grand and atmospheric yet affordable, Budapest is famed for thermal baths, ruin bars, and a vibrant expat scene split across the Danube. It's a longtime nomad favorite for value and nightlife.
vibe_summary: Faded-grandeur Danube city of thermal baths and legendary ruin bars.
tax_notes: A flat 15% personal rate and the EU's lowest 9% corporate tax make Hungary appealing for entrepreneurs.

name: Reykjavik
description: The world's northernmost capital is a tiny, design-savvy basecamp for surreal volcanic landscapes, geothermal pools, and the northern lights. Safe and creative, but remote and very expensive.
vibe_summary: Quirky, creative far-north town ringed by volcanoes, glaciers, and hot springs.
tax_notes: High Nordic-level income taxes and a very high cost of living, with a long-stay remote-work visa available.

name: Dublin
description: An English-speaking European tech capital, Dublin hosts the EU HQs of Google, Meta, and more, with a famously social pub culture. Jobs and connectivity are strong, though housing is brutally tight and costly.
vibe_summary: Friendly, literary tech-boom city where pub craic meets Big Tech offices.
tax_notes: High personal and capital-gains taxes, but a famous 12.5% corporate rate draws global tech HQs.

name: Amalfi Coast
description: A vertiginous stretch of pastel cliffside villages above the Tyrrhenian Sea, the Amalfi Coast is pure Mediterranean glamour. Gorgeous and romantic, though steep, seasonal, and pricey for daily living.
vibe_summary: Lemon-scented pastel villages clinging to cliffs above a sparkling sea.
tax_notes: Standard high Italian taxes, though a flat-tax regime for new wealthy residents exists nationally.

name: Bologna
description: Home to Europe's oldest university and Italy's richest food tradition, Bologna is a porticoed, youthful city in the heart of the country. Central rail links and a student-driven energy make it lively and affordable by Italian standards.
vibe_summary: Red-brick porticoed food capital crackling with student life.
tax_notes: High Italian taxes and slow bureaucracy, partly offset by impatriate tax breaks for new residents.

name: Florence
description: The Renaissance capital packs unmatched art and architecture into a compact, walkable Tuscan center. Beautiful and cultured, it draws students and creatives despite heavy tourism.
vibe_summary: An open-air Renaissance museum of domes, art, and Tuscan light.
tax_notes: High Italian taxes, with impatriate regimes offering relief to qualifying new residents.

name: Lake Como
description: A glamorous alpine lake of villa-lined shores beneath the foothills of the Alps, Lake Como is a serene, upscale retreat near Milan. Stunning scenery and tranquility come with high prices and a quiet pace.
vibe_summary: Mirror-still alpine lake fringed by grand villas and mountain backdrops.
tax_notes: Standard high Italian taxation, with a flat-tax regime nationally available to wealthy new residents.

name: Milan
description: Italy's financial and fashion capital is its most modern, fast-paced, and internationally connected city. Strong jobs, design culture, and Alps access offset higher costs and a less classically pretty core.
vibe_summary: Sleek, fast-moving fashion-and-finance capital with northern polish.
tax_notes: High Italian taxes, though Milan is the prime destination for Italy's impatriate and flat-tax incentives.

name: Naples
description: Chaotic, intense, and gloriously alive, Naples is the birthplace of pizza and a gateway to the islands, Pompeii, and the Amalfi Coast. Affordable and fiercely characterful, with grit to match its beauty.
vibe_summary: Loud, soulful bayside city of pizza, espresso, and unfiltered Italian life.
tax_notes: High Italian taxes, but southern Italy offers extra impatriate tax breaks to attract new residents.

name: Puglia
description: The sun-baked heel of Italy is famed for whitewashed towns, trulli houses, olive groves, and long Adriatic and Ionian coastlines. Slow, scenic, and affordable, it's ideal for laid-back coastal living.
vibe_summary: Whitewashed sun-drenched south of olive groves, trulli, and two seas.
tax_notes: High Italian taxes, with southern-Italy impatriate breaks offering a 90% income exemption to qualifying new residents.

name: Rome
description: An open-air museum where 2,000 years of history sit beside aperitivo bars and buzzing piazzas. Young people come for the cinematic lifestyle, world-class food, and a slow, sun-soaked rhythm that rewards aimless wandering.
vibe_summary: Ancient ruins and golden-hour Vespa rides over plates of cacio e pepe.
tax_notes: High Italian taxes, though a flat-tax regime for new residents and impatriate breaks can sharply cut the bill for qualifying expats.

name: Riga
description: The Baltic's largest city pairs gorgeous Art Nouveau streets with a low cost of living and a quietly growing tech scene. Young remote workers find affordable rent, fast internet, and an under-the-radar nightlife.
vibe_summary: Art Nouveau facades, cheap craft beer, and long summer-night riverside parties.
tax_notes: Moderate EU taxes with a unique corporate model taxing only distributed profits, useful for company owners.

name: Vilnius
description: A compact, green capital with a charming old town and one of Europe's most dynamic fintech and startup scenes. It's affordable, walkable, and increasingly popular with nomads who want EU access without the price tag.
vibe_summary: Baroque old town meets a bohemian breakaway 'republic' and a booming fintech crowd.
tax_notes: Relatively low flat income tax and competitive corporate rates make Lithuania friendly for EU-based founders.

name: Montenegro Coast
description: A dramatic Adriatic coastline of fjord-like bays, medieval stone towns, and yacht-filled marinas. It draws young nomads with cheap living, easy residency, and Mediterranean beauty without the Western European prices.
vibe_summary: Kotor's mountain-wrapped bay, beach clubs, and superyachts at a Balkan price.
tax_notes: Low flat tax rates and easy company setup make Montenegro a rising tax-efficient base for nomads.

name: Amsterdam
description: A canal-laced, bike-first city that's liberal, international, and endlessly social. Young professionals love the world-class English fluency, design-forward culture, and one of Europe's strongest tech job markets.
vibe_summary: Canals, bikes, brown cafés, and a famously open, English-speaking crowd.
tax_notes: High Dutch taxes, partly offset for some expats by the 30% ruling on qualifying employment income.

name: Oslo
description: A clean, affluent fjord-side capital surrounded by forests and ski trails. It's ultra-safe and outdoorsy, drawing young professionals who prize nature access, design, and a high quality of life despite steep prices.
vibe_summary: Fjord swims in summer, ski trails by tram, and Scandinavian minimalism everywhere.
tax_notes: High overall tax burden funds extensive public services; wealth tax adds a layer for higher-net-worth residents.

name: Krakow
description: A stunning medieval city with one of Europe's best-preserved old towns and a thriving student and tech crowd. Cheap, walkable, and packed with cellar bars, it's a favorite affordable base for young nomads.
vibe_summary: Gothic squares and underground vodka bars at unbeatable Central-European prices.
tax_notes: Moderate rates with lower brackets for many earners; Poland offers attractive flat-tax options for some self-employed.

name: Warsaw
description: Poland's fast-rising capital blends a rebuilt old town with gleaming towers and a serious startup economy. It offers big-city energy, strong salaries, and excellent value for young professionals.
vibe_summary: Glass skyscrapers, rebuilt history, and a hard-charging Central-European tech boom.
tax_notes: Moderate Polish taxes with favorable flat-tax and lump-sum regimes available to many self-employed nomads.

name: Algarve
description: Portugal's sun-drenched southern coast of golden cliffs, surf beaches, and whitewashed towns. With cheap living and 300 days of sun, it's a magnet for remote workers and beach-loving nomads.
vibe_summary: Cliff-backed beaches, surf breaks, and laid-back coastal towns drenched in sun.
tax_notes: Standard Portuguese rates are high, but residency programs and prior NHR-style breaks have long drawn expats.

name: Ericeira
description: A whitewashed fishing village turned World Surfing Reserve, with world-class waves on its doorstep. It's a tight-knit, low-key hub for surfers and remote workers seeking ocean-first living near Lisbon.
vibe_summary: World-class surf, cliffside cafés, and a small but devoted nomad-surfer tribe.
tax_notes: Same Portuguese tax framework as the rest of the country, with residency incentives historically appealing to expats.

name: Lisbon
description: Portugal's sunlit, hilly capital is one of the world's top digital-nomad hubs, packed with coworking spaces, startups, and a huge international community. Trams, tiled facades, and Atlantic light make it both beautiful and buzzing.
vibe_summary: Pastel hills, vintage trams, rooftop sunsets, and Europe's densest nomad scene.
tax_notes: High headline rates softened for many newcomers by residency and innovation-focused tax incentives.

name: Madeira
description: A lush volcanic Atlantic island of dramatic cliffs, hiking trails, and year-round spring weather. Its Digital Nomad Village put it on the map as a tight-knit, nature-rich remote-work base.
vibe_summary: Misty levada hikes, ocean cliffs, and a famous purpose-built nomad village.
tax_notes: Madeira's special tax zone offers reduced corporate rates, and Portugal's residency incentives appeal to nomads.

name: Porto
description: Lisbon's atmospheric northern rival, draped over the Douro with port-wine cellars and a granite old town. It's cheaper, more authentic, and increasingly favored by nomads who want charm without the crowds.
vibe_summary: Port-wine cellars, riverside azulejos, and a moodier, cheaper alternative to Lisbon.
tax_notes: Standard Portuguese taxation with residency-based incentives that have made the country a long-time expat favorite.

name: Belgrade
description: A gritty, energetic capital famous for legendary nightlife, riverboat clubs, and rock-bottom prices. Its visa-friendly stance and low costs make it a rising hub for nomads who want big-city buzz on a budget.
vibe_summary: Floating river clubs, brutalist edges, and Europe's most unfiltered nightlife.
tax_notes: Low flat-style rates and lenient visa policies make Serbia a tax-efficient, easy-entry base for nomads.

name: Lake Bled
description: A storybook alpine lake with an island church, clifftop castle, and the Julian Alps as backdrop. It's a serene, outdoorsy retreat for nomads who want nature, hiking, and clean mountain air over city buzz.
vibe_summary: An emerald lake with an island church, ringed by Alps and fairy-tale calm.
tax_notes: Slovenian taxes are on the higher side, typical of well-run EU welfare states.

name: Ljubljana
description: A green, walkable mini-capital with a car-free riverside core, vibrant cafe culture, and the Alps and Adriatic both within reach. It's safe, charming, and a relaxed base for nature-loving remote workers.
vibe_summary: Dragon bridges, riverside cafés, and a car-free old town between Alps and sea.
tax_notes: Moderate-to-high EU taxation funding strong public services and infrastructure.

name: Barcelona
description: A sun-soaked Mediterranean metropolis where Gaudí architecture meets city beaches and a world-famous nightlife. It's one of Europe's top nomad cities, with a huge international scene and a serious startup ecosystem.
vibe_summary: Beachfront bars, Gaudí curves, and all-night terraces under Mediterranean sun.
tax_notes: High Spanish taxes, though the Beckham Law offers qualifying new arrivals a favorable flat rate for several years.

name: Canary Islands
description: A volcanic Atlantic archipelago with eternal spring weather, surf, and a booming nomad scene on islands like Tenerife and Gran Canaria. Low-tax status and year-round sun make it a remote-work paradise.
vibe_summary: Black-sand beaches, year-round spring, and surf-and-laptop island living.
tax_notes: The special Canary Islands regime offers very low corporate tax, a notable draw for company-owning nomads.

name: Málaga
description: The sunny capital of the Costa del Sol has reinvented itself as a tech and nomad hotspot, pairing beaches with a revitalized art-filled center. Warm winters and a fast-growing scene make it a Mediterranean favorite.
vibe_summary: Costa del Sol beaches, a buzzy art-and-tech revival, and 300 days of sun.
tax_notes: Standard high Spanish rates, with the Beckham regime available to qualifying inbound workers.

name: Seville
description: The soulful heart of Andalusia, with Moorish palaces, flamenco, and orange-tree plazas. Its passionate street life and warmth draw young people who want authentic Spanish culture over coastal resort vibes.
vibe_summary: Flamenco nights, tapas crawls, and Moorish courtyards under an Andalusian blaze.
tax_notes: High Spanish taxation, partially mitigated for some new residents under the Beckham Law.

name: Valencia
description: A relaxed beach city blending old-town charm with futuristic architecture and Europe's best urban park. Cheaper than Barcelona but just as sunny, it's surged into a top nomad pick for value and lifestyle.
vibe_summary: Paella's birthplace: city beaches, a futuristic arts complex, and an easy pace.
tax_notes: Spain's high rates apply, with the Beckham regime offering relief to qualifying inbound professionals.

name: Stockholm
description: A polished archipelago capital that's a global unicorn factory and design powerhouse. Young professionals come for the booming tech scene, near-perfect English, and a clean, progressive quality of life.
vibe_summary: Island-hopping city living, summer lake swims, and a world-class startup engine.
tax_notes: Among the world's highest personal taxes, funding expansive public services and a strong safety net.

name: Geneva
description: A lakeside global hub of diplomacy, finance, and watchmaking, ringed by the Alps and Mont Blanc. It draws high earners with top safety, pristine surroundings, and an ultra-international crowd.
vibe_summary: Lake Geneva calm, Alpine weekends, and a polished, multilingual diplomatic set.
tax_notes: No federal capital-gains tax on private wealth and moderate income rates, though living costs are extreme.

name: Zurich
description: Switzerland's wealthy financial and tech capital, consistently topping quality-of-life rankings. It offers immaculate safety, lake-and-Alps access, and some of the world's highest salaries for ambitious professionals.
vibe_summary: Spotless streets, lake swims, Alpine train escapes, and serious banking money.
tax_notes: Moderate income tax and no private capital-gains tax make Switzerland attractive for the wealthy despite high costs.

name: Edinburgh
description: Scotland's dramatic capital of castles, crags, and festivals, with a strong university and tech presence. It's compact, walkable, and culturally rich, drawing young people for its history and creative energy.
vibe_summary: Gothic skyline, festival crowds, and misty hill walks straight from the city.
tax_notes: UK taxes are high, and Scotland's rates run slightly above the rest of Britain for higher earners.

name: London
description: One of the world's great global cities, a finance, culture, and startup heavyweight with unmatched diversity. Young professionals come for the careers, nightlife, and endless cultural depth, despite punishing costs.
vibe_summary: Endless neighborhoods, global ambition, and a museum-and-pub for every mood.
tax_notes: High UK income tax and steep living costs, with recent reforms narrowing the old non-dom advantages.

name: Madrid
description: Spain's grand, high-energy capital with world-class museums, endless terraces, and legendary late-night culture. It blends serious career opportunity with a uniquely social, sun-filled lifestyle.
vibe_summary: Late dinners, all-night terrazas, grand boulevards, and unbeatable street energy.
tax_notes: Madrid levies no regional wealth tax and offers the Beckham regime, making it Spain's most tax-friendly major city.

name: Valletta
description: A tiny golden-stone capital on a sun-baked Mediterranean island that's English-speaking and EU-based. Its iGaming and blockchain industries, plus easy residency, draw a steady stream of remote workers.
vibe_summary: Honey-colored Baroque streets, harbor swims, and English-speaking island life.
tax_notes: Special non-dom and remittance-based regimes can make Malta highly tax-efficient for foreign-income earners.

name: Sofia
description: Bulgaria's affordable capital sits at the foot of Vitosha mountain, blending Soviet-era grit with a fast-growing tech scene. Rock-bottom costs and a flat 10% tax make it a quietly compelling nomad base.
vibe_summary: A mountain on the doorstep, cheap living, and a low-tax, rising tech crowd.
tax_notes: Bulgaria's flat 10% income and corporate tax is among the EU's lowest, a major draw for nomads and founders.

name: Bansko
description: A Pirin-mountain ski town that reinvented itself as one of Europe's cheapest nomad hubs, anchored by a famous coworking space and annual nomad festival. It pairs winter skiing with summer hiking and a tight community.
vibe_summary: Cheap powder days, alpine hikes, and a coworking-driven nomad village vibe.
tax_notes: Same flat 10% Bulgarian tax regime, making this mountain hub one of the cheapest low-tax bases in the EU.

name: Bucharest
description: Romania's sprawling capital pairs Belle Epoque boulevards with a fast-growing tech scene and some of the cheapest big-city living in the EU. Cheap rent, fast internet and a buzzing nightlife make it a quiet favorite for remote workers who want EU access without Western prices.
vibe_summary: Gritty, green and underrated — communist blocks give way to leafy parks, rooftop bars and 1 Gbps fiber.
tax_notes: Romania's flat 10% income tax and low rates make it one of the most tax-friendly spots in the EU for remote earners.

name: Cluj-Napoca
description: Transylvania's youthful university and tech hub, Cluj punches far above its size with a dense startup scene and a big student population. Affordable, walkable and close to the Apuseni mountains, it's a relaxed base for nomads who want nature on the doorstep.
vibe_summary: A compact, student-fueled tech town where medieval squares meet co-working spaces and the mountains start at the city edge.
tax_notes: Romania's flat 10% personal tax and IT-sector incentives make Cluj attractive for tech freelancers and founders.

name: Bratislava
description: A compact capital on the Danube just an hour from Vienna, Bratislava offers eurozone stability and a walkable old town at lower prices than its neighbors. Its size keeps it cozy, while strong rail links open up Central Europe for weekend trips.
vibe_summary: A small, easygoing Danube capital with a fairy-tale old town, castle views and Vienna a short train ride away.
tax_notes: Slovakia's flat-leaning 19% bracket and eurozone membership keep taxes moderate and predictable for residents.

name: Gdansk
description: A handsomely rebuilt Hanseatic port on the Baltic, Gdansk blends colorful old-town streets with sandy beaches and a growing IT sector. Affordable and laid-back, it's a summer-loving base for nomads who want coastline without Mediterranean prices.
vibe_summary: Amber-lit merchant facades, Baltic beaches and a shipyard-born indie spirit, all at easygoing Polish prices.
tax_notes: Poland's lower 12-17% bracket plus a flat-tax option for IT freelancers makes it workable for remote earners.

name: Hamburg
description: Germany's wealthy port city is a hub of media, music and maritime trade, with a famously liberal, creative streak. Canals, red-brick warehouses and a legendary nightlife district give it an outsized cultural pull for a northern climate.
vibe_summary: Maritime grit meets monied cool — canals, the Reeperbahn's neon and a thriving indie-music underground.
tax_notes: Germany's high marginal rates and dense bureaucracy make it costly for high earners, though public services are excellent.

name: Cologne
description: A warm-hearted Rhineland city famous for its Gothic cathedral, riotous Carnival and one of Europe's most open LGBTQ+ scenes. Younger and more laid-back than its size suggests, it's a friendly, well-connected base in western Germany.
vibe_summary: Cathedral spires over Carnival confetti — a beery, open-armed Rhine city that takes fun seriously.
tax_notes: Germany's high income and corporate taxes apply, partly offset by strong healthcare and infrastructure.

name: Rotterdam
description: Europe's largest port reborn as a playground of bold modern architecture and an experimental design and food scene. Flat, bikeable and refreshingly unpretentious, Rotterdam is the Netherlands' most forward-looking young city.
vibe_summary: Skyline-of-the-future architecture, cargo-crane horizons and a maker's energy — Amsterdam's edgier, cheaper sibling.
tax_notes: Dutch taxes are high but English-friendly admin and the (shrinking) expat ruling can ease the burden for newcomers.

name: Gothenburg
description: Sweden's second city is a friendly, design-savvy port with a celebrated food scene and easy access to a wild archipelago. Greener and warmer than its reputation, it offers Nordic quality of life with a slightly more relaxed, affordable feel than Stockholm.
vibe_summary: Cozy 'fika' culture, fresh-seafood obsession and an archipelago of skerries minutes from a walkable, tram-laced core.
tax_notes: Sweden's high personal taxes fund world-class services, but marginal rates above 50% sting top earners.

name: Sarajevo
description: A mountain-ringed capital where minarets, church spires and synagogues share narrow Ottoman lanes, Sarajevo is one of Europe's most atmospheric and affordable cities. Resilient and warm, it draws curious nomads with cheap living, great coffee and ski slopes minutes away.
vibe_summary: East-meets-West in a valley of mosques and cafes, with Olympic ski peaks rising straight from the old town.
tax_notes: Bosnia's flat 10% rates are very low, though navigating its complex administrative system can be slow.

name: Mostar
description: Famous for its arched Ottoman bridge over the turquoise Neretva, Mostar is a small Herzegovinian town of stone lanes, warm summers and Mediterranean light. Cheap and unhurried, it suits slow-traveling nomads who want history and easy day trips to the Adriatic.
vibe_summary: A sun-baked stone town where divers leap from a centuries-old bridge into emerald river water.
tax_notes: Bosnia's flat 10% tax regime is among Europe's lowest, offset by limited services and slow bureaucracy.

name: Antwerp
description: Belgium's diamond and fashion capital is a stylish port city with grand Flemish architecture and an outsized creative reputation. Compact, cultured and brimming with bars and boutiques, it's a chic, well-connected base in the heart of Western Europe.
vibe_summary: Avant-garde fashion, baroque guildhalls and a Belgian-beer cafe culture packed into one dapper river port.
tax_notes: Belgium has high income taxes but notably levies no general capital gains tax on private investors.

name: Jerusalem
description: An ancient, layered city of immense religious and historical weight that draws young scholars, volunteers, and the spiritually curious. Stone alleys and millennia of history sit beside a surprisingly young student energy from its universities.
vibe_summary: Sacred, intense, and weighty — a place where history presses in from every wall.
tax_notes: Israel taxes residents on worldwide income with progressive rates up to 50%, making it moderately tax-heavy for expats.

name: Tel Aviv
description: A beachfront tech powerhouse with a non-stop nightlife and an outsized startup density that earned it the 'Silicon Wadi' nickname. Young professionals love the Mediterranean beaches, café culture, and famously open, liberal atmosphere.
vibe_summary: Sun, surf, and startups — a 24-hour Mediterranean party town that codes by day.
tax_notes: Israeli residents face progressive income tax up to 50% on worldwide income, so the tax burden is high relative to Gulf neighbors.

name: Amman
description: A stable, hilly capital that serves as a calm and affordable base for exploring Petra, the Dead Sea, and Wadi Rum. A growing café and startup scene plus warm hospitality make it a comfortable Middle Eastern entry point for young expats.
vibe_summary: Whitewashed hills, mezze, and easygoing hospitality at a gateway to ancient wonders.
tax_notes: Jordan has moderate progressive income tax up to 30% but exempts most capital gains, making it reasonably friendly for investors.

name: Abu Dhabi
description: The UAE's wealthy, more measured capital, blending sleek modern towers, cultural megaprojects like the Louvre, and tax-free salaries. Cleaner and quieter than Dubai, it appeals to professionals who want Gulf money without the full frenzy.
vibe_summary: Polished, planned, and tax-free — petrodollar calm with a growing arts ambition.
tax_notes: The UAE levies zero personal income tax and no capital gains tax, with only a 9% corporate rate on larger profits.

name: Dubai
description: A glittering, hyper-modern megacity that has become a magnet for digital nomads and entrepreneurs chasing tax-free income, luxury, and superb connectivity. Beaches, brunches, skyscrapers, and a huge international community make it the Gulf's headline destination.
vibe_summary: Tax-free, sun-soaked ambition — supercars, skyline brunches, and a global hustle.
tax_notes: Dubai charges no personal income or capital gains tax and offers a dedicated remote-work visa, making it a top tax haven for nomads.

name: Sharjah
description: The UAE's conservative cultural capital, offering cheaper rent than neighboring Dubai while keeping the same tax-free income. A dry emirate focused on museums, heritage, and family life rather than nightlife.
vibe_summary: Quieter, cheaper, and more traditional — the heritage-minded side of the Gulf.
tax_notes: As part of the UAE, Sharjah has no personal income or capital gains tax, though it is a strictly alcohol-free emirate.

name: Beirut
description: A resilient, hedonistic Mediterranean city famous for legendary nightlife, food, and a fiercely creative cultural scene despite ongoing economic turmoil. Beaches, mountains, and a worldly, French-influenced crowd give it an intensity few cities match.
vibe_summary: Battered but unbreakable — Mediterranean glamour, all-night parties, and raw creative fire.
tax_notes: Lebanon has relatively low tax rates, but severe banking and currency dysfunction make financial life unpredictable for expats.

name: Kuwait City
description: A wealthy, oil-rich Gulf capital with tax-free salaries, modern malls, and a strong expat workforce. Conservative and dry, it draws professionals more for high earnings than lifestyle, though its waterfront and food scene have their fans.
vibe_summary: Tax-free Gulf earning power wrapped in malls, heat, and a quiet conservative pace.
tax_notes: Kuwait imposes no personal income tax on individuals, making take-home pay very high despite a conservative, alcohol-free environment.

name: Ras Al Khaimah
description: The UAE's adventure emirate, home to the country's highest mountain, zip-lines, and quieter beaches at a far lower cost than Dubai. Tax-free and laid-back, it suits outdoorsy expats who want Gulf perks without the big-city crush.
vibe_summary: Jebel Jais peaks, empty beaches, and tax-free calm — the UAE's outdoor escape.
tax_notes: As an emirate of the UAE, Ras Al Khaimah charges zero personal income and capital gains tax with a low 9% corporate rate.

name: Barbados
description: A Caribbean island that launched a dedicated 12-month remote-work visa, drawing a steady stream of digital nomads to its beaches and rum shacks. English-speaking and laid-back, it pairs world-class surf and snorkeling with a growing co-working scene.
vibe_summary: Turquoise water, flying-fish lunches, and a slow island rhythm where the office is a beachfront balcony.
tax_notes: Remote-work visa holders are not taxed locally on foreign income, and there is no capital gains tax.

name: Montreal
description: A bilingual, artsy metropolis with European bones, cheap-by-North-American-standards rent, and a legendary festival and nightlife calendar. Its universities keep the city young, creative, and packed with affordable studios and cafes.
vibe_summary: French-Canadian cool: jazz festivals, bagels at 3am, and a creative scene that punches far above the cost of living.
tax_notes: Combined federal and Quebec rates are among the highest in North America, with capital gains partially taxed as income.

name: Toronto
description: Canada's economic engine and one of the most diverse cities on earth, with a booming tech sector and endless international food. Young professionals come for the jobs and the cosmopolitan energy, despite steep rents.
vibe_summary: A polished, hyper-diverse global city where a thousand cultures share patios along the lake.
tax_notes: High combined federal-Ontario marginal rates apply, though only 50% of capital gains are typically taxable.

name: Vancouver
description: Wedged between ocean and mountains, Vancouver is North America's outdoor capital, with skiing, surfing, and hiking all within an hour. Mild winters and a Pacific-Rim food scene make it a magnet for active young professionals willing to pay for the lifestyle.
vibe_summary: Mountains meet sea: ski in the morning, paddleboard by afternoon, ramen by night.
tax_notes: BC's high marginal rates bite, but no provincial sales tax quirks and only half of capital gains are taxable.

name: San José
description: The Central Valley capital is Costa Rica's hub for remote workers seeking the 'pura vida' lifestyle with city conveniences and a temperate, spring-like climate year round. It serves as a base for nomads exploring the country's beaches and rainforest.
vibe_summary: Eternal-spring valley city, gateway to volcanoes and surf, with a relaxed pura-vida pace.
tax_notes: Costa Rica taxes only local-source income, so foreign nomad earnings generally escape local tax under the digital-nomad visa.

name: Tamarindo
description: A Pacific surf town in Guanacaste that has grown into a major digital-nomad and expat hub, with reliable waves, beach cafes, and co-working spaces. It offers the pura-vida beach lifestyle with enough infrastructure to actually work.
vibe_summary: Sun-baked surf town where dawn patrol and afternoon Zoom calls coexist over fresh ceviche.
tax_notes: Costa Rica's territorial tax system means foreign-earned income is generally untaxed for nomad-visa holders.

name: Havana
description: A time-capsule capital of vintage cars, crumbling colonial grandeur, and an extraordinary live-music culture. It captivates culturally curious travelers, though unreliable internet and infrastructure make it a tough place to actually work remotely.
vibe_summary: Faded grandeur and son cubano spilling from doorways onto the Malecón at sunset.
tax_notes: Cuba's state-controlled economy and currency complications make formal tax engagement rare and impractical for short-stay nomads.

name: Curaçao
description: A Dutch-Caribbean island below the hurricane belt with technicolor Willemstad architecture, world-class diving, and a remote-work visa. Its arid climate, calm seas, and multilingual locals make it an underrated nomad base.
vibe_summary: Pastel Dutch colonial waterfront, desert-dry skies, and reef diving straight off the shore.
tax_notes: The @Home remote-work permit lets foreign-income nomads stay without triggering local income tax, and there is no capital gains tax.

name: Punta Cana
description: A resort strip on the DR's eastern tip with palm-lined white-sand beaches and a growing community of remote workers priced out of pricier islands. It's all-inclusive tourism on the surface with affordable long-stay rentals just inland.
vibe_summary: Endless coconut palms, warm turquoise shallows, and resort-town ease at Caribbean-budget prices.
tax_notes: The DR taxes on a territorial basis, so foreign-earned nomad income is generally not taxed locally.

name: Santo Domingo
description: The oldest European-founded city in the Americas, with a UNESCO colonial zone, lively merengue nightlife, and big-city affordability. It offers more culture, jobs, and infrastructure than the DR's resort towns.
vibe_summary: Cobblestone colonial quarter by day, bachata-soaked malecón by night, all on a Caribbean budget.
tax_notes: Territorial taxation means foreign income earned by nomads is generally outside the Dominican tax net.

name: Antigua
description: A jaw-droppingly photogenic colonial town ringed by volcanoes, with cobblestone streets, Spanish schools, and a buzzing cafe-and-co-working scene. Cheap living and spring-like weather make it a favorite first stop for nomads in Latin America.
vibe_summary: Pastel colonial facades framed by smoking volcanoes, with the best coffee culture in Central America.
tax_notes: Guatemala's low personal rates and territorial system make it light-touch for foreign-income nomads.

name: Cabo San Lucas
